title: Data Injection Vulnerability
description: Due to the lack of information regarding input validation and sanitization, the system is vulnerable to data injection attacks. Attackers can inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting) into input fields, potentially altering data, executing arbitrary commands, or compromising the system.
attack_vector: Malicious input crafted by an attacker, bypassing input validation and sanitization routines.  This could involve injecting SQL commands, JavaScript code, or other harmful payloads.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization for all user inputs.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.
